spineRead.rawText: THE GREEN AMENDMENT — MAYA K. VAN ROSSUM
spineRead.title: THE GREEN AMENDMENT
spineRead.author: MAYA K. VAN ROSSUM

books.title: The Green Amendment the People's Fight for a Clean, Safe, and Healthy Environment
books.author: Maya K. van Rossum
books.authorLF: Rossum, Maya K. van; Ruffalo, Mark; Harris, Kerri Evelyn
books.reasoning: [social_political] LCC KF3775 places this in environmental law. The subtitle 'The People's Fight for a Clean, Safe, and Healthy Environment' and synopsis describing a 'roadmap' for environmental activists clearly indicate this covers both environmental law and activism. The author is described as a 'veteran environmentalist' providing strategy for activists, supporting both proposed tags. | [science] LCC KF3775 indicates environmental law, which falls outside the Science & mathematics domain (Q). However, the subject headings include 'Environmental Conservation & Protection' and 'Nature', and the synopsis describes environmental activism and policy. This appears to be environmental science/policy rather than pure law, justifying the proposed tag within the Science & mathematics domain.
warnings: LCC code missing — tags inferred from title and author only.
books.canonicalTitle: The Green Amendment The People's Fight for a Clean, Safe, and Healthy Environment
books.subtitle: The People's Fight for a Clean, Safe, and Healthy Environment
books.synopsis:
A veteran environmentalist shares her roadmap to a healthier world—one that uses the law to empower activists and provide hope for communities everywhere.


We have reached a critical tipping point in our fight for the environment: Corporations profit off climate change, natural disasters devastate homes, and the most vulnerable suffer the health effects of pollution. Yet our laws are designed to accommodate this destruction rather than prevent it. Without government support, it's no wonder people feel powerless. But there is a solution.


In
The Green Amendment, veteran environmentalist Maya K. van Rossum presents her radically simple plan for a green future: bypass local laws and turn to the ultimate authority—our state and federal constitutions—to ensure we have the right to a healthy environment.


Through compelling interviews with activists on the ground, clear evidence from experts, and heartbreaking stories from those hit hardest by environmental ruin,
The Green Amendment lights the path forward. In this updated edition of her trailblazing 2017 book, van Rossum invites readers to join the movement by sharing:



  • Why Green Amendments work where other movements have failed

  • How to position Green Amendments and what specific language offers the strongest legal protections

  • How to argue in favor of environmental rights, and the economic and health benefits that will help activists make the case

  • How Green Amendments address the crucial intersection of environmentalism and anti-racism

  • What everyone—from artists and students to scientists and lawyer—can do to further the cause


With the power of
The Green Amendment, we can claim our environmental rights, ensuring a clean, safe Earth for generations to come.

original.title: The Green Amendment the People's Fight for a Clean, Safe, and Healthy Environment
original.author: Maya K. van Rossum

books.title: Crime and Punishment
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.reasoning: [literature] Classic Russian novel by Dostoevsky clearly fits Fiction. LCC PG indicates Slavic literature, and this is a foundational work of world literature. Subject headings confirm Fiction and Literary classification. | [psychology] This is Dostoevsky's Crime and Punishment, a classic Russian novel. The LCC classification PG (Slavic literature) and DDC 891.73 (Russian fiction) clearly indicate this is literature, not psychology. The subject heading 'Psychological' likely refers to psychological realism in fiction. Since the domain is settled as Psychology, I can only propose a psychological fiction tag, but this book doesn't belong in the Psychology domain at all.
books.synopsis:
Hailed by Washington Post Book World as “the best [translation] currently available" when it was first published, this second edition of Crime and Punishment has been updated in honor of the 200th anniversary of Dostoevsky’s birth. • ONE OF TIME MAGAZINE'S 100 BEST MYSTERY AND THRILLER BOOKS OF ALL TIME

With the same suppleness, energy, and range of voices that won their translation of The Brothers Karamazov the PEN/Book-of-the-Month Club Prize, Richard Pevear and Larissa Volokhonsky offer a brilliant translation of Crime and Punishment, Dostoevsky's astounding pyschological thriller, newly revised for his bicentenniel. 

In Crime and Punishment, when Raskolnikov, an impoverished student living in the St. Petersburg of the tsars, commits an act of murder and theft, he sets into motion a story that is almost unequalled in world literature for its excruciating suspense, its atmospheric vividness, and its depth of characterization and vision. Dostoevsky’s drama of sin, guilt, and redemption transforms the sordid story of an old woman’s murder into the nineteenth century’s profoundest and most compelling philosophical novel.

books.title: Bell Curve
books.author: Richard J. Herrnstein
books.reasoning: [psychology] LCC BF 431 places this in differential psychology/intelligence testing. The book is a major work on IQ research and psychometric testing, examining intelligence differences across populations. These are core psychology subdisciplines that merit specific tags. | [social_political] The Bell Curve is a highly controversial work examining the relationship between intelligence, race, and social outcomes. The synopsis explicitly mentions linking intelligence to class and race, making it a clear case of social criticism with racial intelligence themes. LCC BF431 (individual differences in psychology) supports the intelligence focus, while the policy implications described make it social criticism.

books.title: Slide:ology
books.author: Nancy Duarte

The subtitle 'The Art and Science of Creating Great Presentations' and publisher O'Reilly clearly indicate this is a how-to guide. The subject matter doesn't fit existing Media/tech tags well, so proposing specific presentation design tag. | [social_political] This is a business presentation guide (LCC HF5718.22 = business communication), which falls outside the Social & political domain. The subtitle 'The Art and Science of Creating Great Presentations' and synopsis confirm it's instructional content, warranting the How-to / guide form tag. However, no genre tags from the Social & political domain apply to this business/communication topic.
books.subtitle: The Art and Science of Creating Great Presentations
books.synopsis: No matter where you are on the organizational ladder, the odds are high that you've delivered a high-stakes presentation to your peers, your boss, your customers, or the general public. Presentation software is one of the few tools that requires professionals to think visually on an almost daily basis. But unlike verbal skills, effective visual expression is not easy, natural, or actively taught in schools or business training programs. slide:ology fills that void. Written by Nancy Duarte, President and CEO of Duarte Design, the firm that created the presentation for Al Gore's Oscar-winning film, An Inconvenient Truth, this book is full of practical approaches to visual story development that can be applied by anyone. The book combines conceptual thinking and inspirational design, with insightful case studies from the world's leading brands. With slide:ology you'll learn to: Connect with specific audiences Turn ideas into informative graphics Use sketching and diagramming techniques effectively Create graphics that enable audiences to process information easily Develop truly influential presentations Utilize presentation technology to your advantage Millions of presentations and billions of slides have been produced -- and most of them miss the mark. slide:ology will challenge your traditional approach to creating slides by teaching you how to be a visual thinker. And it will help your career by creating momentum for your cause.
